biblical.framework: The king is chosen by God, accountable to God's law, and serves to maintain covenant justice.

conventional.framework: The king (Pharaoh) is a god on earth, unaccountable, whose word is law.
conventional.evidence: Old Kingdom Pyramid Texts, divine status of Horus.

comparisons.resolution: Pharaonic kingship represents the archetypal 'kingdom of man' built on pride, while biblical kingship provides a servant-leader model under divine law.
